Shannon proved that this is the only notion of information that satisfies continuity, monotonicity, and additivity (for independent data).

  • Mutual information: information provided by about

(23)

  • Properties: , i.e. if Y is known, how much is the uncertainty of reduced?

  • Information about X cannot increase through processing. That is, . Layers of a network can only reduce or keep information about its input.

  • Data processing inequality: Consider a Markov chain: . Then .

  • Rate-Distortion Theory:

    Suppose we have a distortion measure . Let be the average number of bits to represent . Define the rate distortion function : What is the minimum number of bits per symbol of you must transmit to achieve that expected distortion? If the bottleneck (in bits) is less than R(D), it is would be impossible to reconstruct with distortion .

Informally we describe information as water and functions as pipes. Water can never increase but only leak out. The narrowest pipe determines the maximum flow.

Adding skip connections changes the story!

11.2. Modeling Information with NNs

Let be observation and be an attribute. I.e. is an image and is the softmax probabilities. Discriminative models model . Generative moedls model . More generally, and can be any variables.

Information theoretic perspective on supervised learning, autoencoding, and contrastive learning.

11.2.1. Supervised learning

View input image as an random variable with a label . from the data processing inequality. Ideally the information discarded is task-irrelevant i.e. . Recall . Cross entropy loss encourages retaining .

11.2.3. [REVIEW] Contrastive learning

Mutual Information Neural Estimation (MINE): Try to approximate the pointwise mutual information with a neural network. Donkser-Varadhan representation gives formula for as an optimization problem in . Hence we can sample of a bunch of data Sample a bunch of data from , you approximate this by shuffling y and then maximize the Donsker Varadhan predictor.

InfoNCE (noisy contrastive estimation) Normalize by L2 norm of embeddings so we assume embeddings lie on the unit sphere. If we didn’t do this then temperature would be effectively meaningless.

Lower temperature makes it more “winner takes all”. Makes training more sensitive.
